title: cordylanthus wrightii subsp. tenuifolius
intro: Cordylanthus wrightii subsp. tenuifolius, also known as Wright's bird's beak or narrow-leaved bird's beak, is a perennial plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly by Native American communities, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cordylanthus wrightii subsp. tenuifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource in herbalism.
